hypothesis: Incorporating explicit electronegativity difference tensors between first-shell and second-shell elements as an additional conditioning signal in the latent diffusion model will improve adsorption energy prediction accuracy and generation fidelity for multi-component catalytic surfaces beyond what is achievable with composition vectors alone, because electronegativity gradients across shells encode charge-transfer-driven ligand effects that are not captured by elemental fractions but directly modulate adsorbate binding strength.
